channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Femi Otedola Increases Stake In FirstHoldCo To 19.36%
item.description: Femi Otedola, the billionaire chairman of First HoldCo Plc, don buy more shares of the company worth about N43.41 billion on May 13, 2026. This move make him own total of 8.6 billion shares, wey now r...

item.title: Brookfield Considers Restarting V.C. Summer Nuclear Plant in South Carolina
item.description: Brookfield Asset Management has been selected by Santee Cooper to take over and potentially complete the unfinished Units 2 and 3 at the Virgil C. Summer Nuclear Station in South Carolina, a project t...

item.title: Marco Rubio enters China via transliteration loophole despite previous sanctions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio accompanied President Donald Trump on a state visit to China in 2026, despite being banned from the country under sanctions imposed in 2020 due to his criticism of Beiji...

item.title: European Defence Expansion Raises Political and Strategic Challenges
item.description: In the wake of Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine and uncertainties surrounding the second Trump administration, Europe has accelerated its military build-up, emphasising the need for strategic autonom...

item.title: Tanzania Plans to Revive Petroleum Exploration and Attract New Investments
item.description: The Ministry of Energy in Tanzania, under the guidance of Permanent Secretary Dr. James Mataragio, is taking steps to revitalize petroleum exploration in the country. During a meeting with the Petrole...

item.title: Ontario Sees Sharp Increase in Ineligible OSAP Payments, but Fraud Remains Low
item.description: The Ontario Student Assistance Program (OSAP) has experienced a significant rise in cases where students received funds ineligible due to misrepresentation, according to documents obtained by Global N...

item.title: Ontario Premier Faces Criticism Over $200K in Additional Private Jet Expenses
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford is facing criticism from political opponents and advocacy groups after it was revealed that taxpayers covered nearly $200,000 in extra costs associated with a brief ownership...

item.title: Pakistan Clarifies Nature of Chinese FM’s Call on Iran Mediation
item.description: The Foreign Office (FO) of Pakistan has clarified that Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi's recent phone call with Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar was misrepresented in some media r...

item.title: Angela Rayner cleared by HMRC over stamp duty payments, paving way for leadership speculation
item.description: Angela Rayner has been cleared of any wrongdoing by HMRC concerning her tax affairs, following the settlement of £40,000 in underpaid stamp duty on her £800,000 flat in Hove. Initially, she paid the s...

item.title: Iran Urges BRICS to Condemn US Actions and Support International Law
item.description: On the opening day of the BRICS Summit held in New Delhi, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi delivered a strong message regarding Iran’s position against what he termed as US 'bullying'. Addressi...

item.title: Mikey Bustos’s AntsCanada Recognized at the 30th Webby Awards
item.description: Filipino-Canadian entertainer Mikey Bustos has once again made waves internationally as his YouTube channel, AntsCanada, received recognition at the 30th Annual Webby Awards. Known for his engaging an...

item.title: Examining the Rising School Dropout Rates Among Kenyan Boys
item.description: Kenya is facing a worrying trend where more boys than girls are dropping out of school despite being a slightly smaller portion of the population. According to a report by Usawa Agenda, five in every ...

item.title: Unauthorized Seizure of Ship Near UAE Raises Tensions with Iran
item.description: A vessel anchored off the east coast of the United Arab Emirates has been reportedly seized and is being directed toward Iranian territorial waters, according to the British military. The United Kingd...

item.title: Escalation in Ukraine as Russia launches drones and missile strikes amid global diplomatic meetings
item.description: On May 14, 2026, Russia intensified its military campaign against Ukraine, deploying both drones and missiles targeting various regions of the country. These strikes have resulted in significant destr...

item.title: Blues forward Kennedy Cherrington penalised for dangerous tackle
item.description: During the recent NRL match, Blues forward Kennedy Cherrington was penalised for performing a crusher tackle, an action considered dangerous under the league's rules. The incident has sparked discussi...

item.title: Witness claims Feroz Khan misled police panel during 2021 cocaine seizure investigation
item.description: A witness at the Madlanga commission of inquiry has alleged that Maj-Gen Feroz Khan, head of police counter-intelligence, misled an internal disciplinary panel led by Lt-Gen Nhlahla Mkhwanazi, which c...

item.title: Kanini Kega Pays Tribute to His Mother Days Before Her Passing
item.description: EALA MP Kanini Kega emotionally recounted the final days of his mother, Annah Ndung'u, who passed away suddenly at the age of 83. Kega described her struggle with dementia, noting that despite occasio...

item.title: AbdulRazaq tells APC aspirants their efforts dey appreciated ahead of Kwara primaries
item.description: Kwara State Governor, AbdulRahman AbdulRazaq, don yan all APC aspirants say dem na winners, even if dem no fit carry ticket for the upcoming primaries. The governor yan this one during meeting with AP...

item.title: FHA begins construction of 195 new housing units in Ogun State under Tinubu’s directive
item.description: For Ogun State, housing dey finally get correct push as Federal Housing Authority (FHA) don start construction of FHA Gateway Estate for Ofada, Obafemi Owode Local Government Area. The groundbreaking ...

item.title: Labor’s tax reforms aim to address intergenerational inequity in property investment
item.description: The 2026 federal budget has sparked debate over changes to capital gains, negative gearing, and trust taxation. These policies, historically favouring older, asset-rich Australians, have been modestly...
